core_mechanism: nonlinear multi-wave mixing (coalescence of several input waves into one output) via a medium's field-induced nonlinear polarizability, computed by resumming the coupling to a strong external driving field exactly to all orders, so the response depends non-analytically (non-perturbatively) on the drive amplitude rather than through a truncated power series
brief_reason: The abstract describes several driving-field quanta merging into one output through the polarization of the medium, with the effect obtained by 'accounting exactly for the laser field' and showing a 'highly non-perturbative dependence on the laser intensity and frequency' — i.e. an all-orders-resummed nonlinear wave-mixing response that is non-analytic in the drive strength.
